Xiamara Review - Empires and Puzzles Strategy Guide

Don't let her elegant antlers fool you; Xiamara is a ticking time bomb of nature damage. While her initial hit might tickle, her second strike extinguishes entire teams, and her unique Mana Corruption punishes anyone greedy enough to boost their mana. Is she still the queen of the woods or just a lost fawn in the power creep forest?
Xiamara: The Forest's Vengeance & Mana Corruption Queen
Xiamara: The Scaling Stag of Destruction
Xiamara is a 5-star Nature (Green) hero from the
Alliance Quest event. Released in late 2023, she belongs to the noble

. In a game dominated by instant gratification, Xiamara asks for a little patience—and rewards it with absolute devastation. She is a "Hit All" damage dealer who functions as a ramping threat; leave her alive long enough to fire twice, and you likely won't have a team left to regret it. She also introduces a unique "Mana Corruption" mechanic that turns your enemies' greed for mana into their downfall.

Xiamara's design is high fantasy at its finest. Adorned in emerald and gold armor with majestic antlers crowning her head, she looks every bit the "Guardian of the Emerald Forest." She holds a massive banner-staff that probably weighs more than most 3-star heroes. The art conveys a sense of regal authority and calm before the storm—which is fitting, considering her special skill starts manageable and ends in a cataclysm. It’s a nice break from the "spiky bits everywhere" aesthetic of the Goblin village.
Xiamara's stats were impressive upon release but now face the harsh reality of late-stage power creep.
When compared to the latest monsters of late 2025 (who boast base stats around
), Xiamara is undeniably showing her age.
Her base attack (1044) is decent but no longer elite.
This is the danger zone. Her defense (935) and Health (1629) are significantly lower than modern standards. A fully Limit Broken (LB2) Xiamara has stats roughly equivalent to a
modern hero. She is "squishy" by today's metric and requires protection (Taunt, Defense Up) to survive long enough to do her job.
Special Skill: Power of the Forest
This skill is a two-part engine of destruction.
The initial 300% hit to all enemies is "okay"—it softens the field. However, the magic happens on the
cast. The caster gets +100% power immediately for the next cast, increasing by 25% per turn up to +200%. This means her second shot deals between
. Even with outdated stats, a 500% AoE hit is usually a team wipe.
Mana Corruption is a fascinating control tool. It deals
(fixed) whenever an enemy receives mana from
sources (buffs, minions, passives). It does NOT trigger on normal tile gain
the enemy has a mana generation buff active. This makes her a hard counter to heavy mana-boosting teams (e.g., Hippos, Ludwig, Waterpipe minions).

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Stag family offers great utility, specifically targeting one of the most annoying debuffs in the game: Attack Down.
This is excellent. Attack Down ailments (from heroes like C.Kadilen, various Barbarians, etc.) are flipped into an
. This ensures Xiamara's second hit isn't neutered by a debuff right before she fires.

Xiamara is a Cleric, and this passive adds to her sustain. 5% health isn't huge, but in a buff-heavy team (e.g., with buffs from herself or allies), it adds up, helping mitigate her lower health pool.

for the first 6 turns is arguably the best Aether Power for her. It guarantees she doesn't get Silenced, Mindless Attacked, or Defense Downed before she can charge her first engine. It protects her "ramp up" phase perfectly.
Upon release, Xiamara was a top-tier damage dealer. Players compared her favorably to
, but with the added benefit of hitting
immediately rather than hitting 3. She was a premier "finisher."
The damage percentages (300% -> 500%) have aged gracefully; 500% to all is still top-tier damage. However, her
have not. She dies much faster now. The Mana Corruption mechanic remains unique and effective against specific modern mana-spamming meta heroes.
She is no longer a "set and forget" defense hero because she is too easy to kill before she fires twice. However, on
, especially in Rush Wars or against passive teams that allow her to charge, she is still a nuclear option. She is a tactical nuke that needs a delivery system (protection).
As of this review, Xiamara does not have a costume.
Xiamara's role is simple: Survive long enough to fire twice. However, her damage potential is her main asset.
Maximizes her lower defense/HP stats to ensure she survives the 10+ turns needed to fire her second devastating shot.
Sacrifices some raw damage on the first hit.
She might die to a stiff breeze or a few stray slash attacks before doing her job.
path is recommended. A dead Xiamara deals 0% damage. Her multipliers are high enough that she will kill things even without full attack emblems, provided she stays alive.
Her second cast is one of the hardest hitting AoE skills in the game.
Punishes mana buffers severely with fixed damage.
Stag family makes her immune to Attack Down, and LB2 makes her immune to everything else for 6 turns.
Passive healing on buffs helps keep her topped up.
Significantly lower stats than modern 2025/2026 heroes make her a liability on defense.
Requires firing twice to reach full potential. In fast metas, the match is often decided before an Average hero fires twice.
Her power boost is a buff that can theoretically be dispelled (though often difficult to time).
This is her playground. At Very Fast speed, she can fire her first and second shots in rapid succession, wiping the enemy team before they can settle in.
Excellent for waves where you can charge her up on easier enemies and unleash a 500% nuke on the boss wave.
Great against "stall" teams or teams relying heavily on mana generation buffs (Hippo, etc.).
You need to build around her. She is the payload; bring the truck to carry her.
A taunt hero protects Xiamara while she charges. Ludwig specifically boosts her mana, helping her cycle to that second cast faster.
Mana boosters help her fire faster. Note that Xnolphod's mana boost works well with her.
Elemental Defense Down allows even her
300% hit to be lethal, removing the need for the second cast entirely.
(Luna, Xia, Bubbles) who stop her from firing.
who can one-shot her due to her lower defense.
who strip her power boost before she casts the second time.
Do not pair her with heroes that overwrite her own buffs with weaker versions, though this is rare for her specific self-buffs.

If you pulled Xiamara, you have a gem. Even with "outdated" stats, she will carry you through almost all Map Stages and Quests. Her AoE damage is fantastic for farming. Prioritize keeping her alive with healers and defense buffers.
She is a luxury tool. Do not put her on defense; she will be hunted. Use her in
or as a specialized counter to heavy mana-buff teams. Is she worth Alpha Aethers?
The Ailment Immunity is crucial for her survival, but if you have the latest 2026 OP hero, the Aethers might be better spent there. She is a solid "B-Team" war hero now.
The community has always had a soft spot for the "Stag scaling" mechanic.
“Enjoy her in this state while you can. Overpowered.”
- A player at launch (October 2023). History proves she wasn't nerfed, but power creep did the job for them.
“I massively overlooked this... it’s for normal mana increase if they have a mana gen buff too... that’s a great secondary skill.”
- A player realizing the potential of her Mana Corruption.
was constant, with Xiamara generally considered the "modern" (at the time) upgrade due to hitting all enemies.

Xiamara is a hero who demands respect but requires a chaperone. She hits like a truck... a truck from 2023, but a truck nonetheless. If you can keep her standing long enough to cycle her special, she remains one of the most satisfying "board clearers" in the game. While the shiny new toys of 2026 might outstat her, few can match the sheer satisfaction of that second "Power of the Forest" cast flattening an entire enemy team.
A powerful but fragile cannon. Keep her polished for Rush Wars and specific counters.
Detailed review of Xiamara, the 5-star Nature hero from the Clash of Knights event. Analysis of her scaling damage, mana corruption mechanic, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
